top_stories.title: Microsoft Entra ID gets passkeys default authentication starting September
top_stories.summary: Microsoft announced that passkeys will become the default authentication method for Entra ID starting September 2026. This represents a significant shift in enterprise authentication defaults for one of the largest IAM platforms, affecting organizations worldwide that rely on Entra ID for identity management.

top_stories.title: OAuth Client ID Spoofing Lets Attackers Validate Stolen Microsoft Entra Credentials
top_stories.summary: A novel evasion technique called OAuth client ID spoofing is being actively weaponized by at least two distinct threat actors to validate stolen credentials in Microsoft Entra ID environments. The technique allows attackers to enumerate user accounts and confirm stolen credentials without generating successful sign-in events, effectively bypassing standard detection telemetry used by defenders.

top_stories.title: Completes Acquisition of Entro Security to Enhance Non-Human Identity Management
top_stories.summary: SailPoint completed its acquisition of Entro Security, a non-human identity (NHI) and credential security specialist, as announced on June 29. The acquisition integrates Entro's NHI capabilities into SailPoint's identity governance platform, expanding its coverage beyond human identities to machine identities and secrets management.

top_stories.title: SailPoint (SAIL) Completes Acquisition of Entro Security to Enhance Non-Human Identity Management
top_stories.summary: SailPoint has completed its acquisition of Entro Security, expanding its identity governance platform to include non-human identity management capabilities. Entro Security specializes in managing secrets, service accounts, and machine identities, addressing a growing gap in traditional IGA solutions.

top_stories.title: New phishing kits target Microsoft 365 accounts, evade MFA
top_stories.summary: Two new phishing kits named Jalisco and OmegaLord have been discovered targeting Microsoft 365 accounts with techniques that bypass multi-factor authentication. The kits represent emerging adversary-in-the-middle or token-theft attack methods that undermine MFA protections on a widely used identity platform.

news.title: Microsoft Patches Record 622 Flaws, Including Two Zero-Days Under Active Attack
news.summary: Microsoft released its largest-ever Patch Tuesday update, addressing 622 CVEs including two zero-day vulnerabilities currently under active exploitation. Incident responders were credited with discovering both actively exploited flaws, which are the priority patches in the release.

news.title: LastPass, Bitwarden users targeted with fake security alerts
news.summary: LastPass is warning its users about an active phishing campaign using fake security alerts to redirect them to fraudulent websites. Bitwarden users are also being targeted in the campaign, which impersonates legitimate security notifications to steal credentials.

news.title: SonicWall SMA appliances targeted in zero-day attacks (CVE-2026-15409, CVE-2026-15410)
news.summary: SonicWall has patched two actively exploited zero-day vulnerabilities (CVE-2026-15409, CVE-2026-15410) in its Secure Mobile Access 1000 Series appliances. Remediation guidance includes upgrading firmware, checking for indicators of compromise, re-imaging affected appliances, changing user and administrator passwords, and resetting TOTP tokens.

news.title: New macOS malware steals passwords by posing as Apple’s crash-reporting tool
news.summary: Jamf Threat Labs discovered a new macOS infostealer called CrashStealer that disguises itself as Apple's crash-reporting tool to steal passwords, Keychain data, and cryptocurrency wallets. First spotted in May 2026 during development, the malware moved into active in-the-wild use by early July, with Jamf noting it is more sophisticated than typical macOS commodity stealers.

news.title: Google adds FIDO2 keys and phone passkeys to Windows login via GCPW
news.summary: Google is rolling out FIDO2 physical security key support as a second factor for authentication in Google Credential Provider for Windows (GCPW) to all Google Workspace customers. The update enables administrators to enforce 2-step verification with hardware security keys for Windows device login using Google Workspace credentials.

news.title: RabbitMQ Flaws Could Leak OAuth Secrets and Expose Cross-Tenant Queue Metadata
news.summary: Two access control vulnerabilities in RabbitMQ have been disclosed that can leak OAuth client secrets and allow cross-tenant queue metadata exposure. The flaws, discovered by Miggo's security team, could enable attackers to bypass tenant boundaries and expose enterprise messaging infrastructure.

news.title: Microsoft Maps Three Salesforce Attack Paths Tied to a Year of ShinyHunters Activity
news.summary: Microsoft has identified three attack paths used by threat actors aligned with ShinyHunters to compromise corporate Salesforce environments over the past year. The intrusions rely on abusing OAuth connections and existing trust relationships between Salesforce and third-party applications rather than exploiting platform vulnerabilities.

news.title: Zero trust workload identity manager version 1.1 generally available on Red Hat OpenShift
news.summary: A zero trust workload identity manager version 1.1 has reached general availability on Red Hat OpenShift. The solution addresses workload identity and access management challenges across multi-cluster, multi-cloud, and hybrid environments, replacing traditional long-lived secrets and static certificates.

news.title: Forg365 PhaaS Targets Microsoft 365 with Device Code and AitM Session Theft
news.summary: A new phishing-as-a-service platform called Forg365 targets Microsoft 365 accounts using device code phishing and adversary-in-the-middle session theft techniques. The service, distributed via Telegram for $400/month, combines AitM tactics, antibot evasion, AI-assisted lure creation, and post-compromise mailbox operations to bypass authentication controls.

news.title: Attacker Uses Suspected AI-Generated PowerShell Script to Map Active Directory
news.summary: An unknown threat actor used a suspected AI-generated PowerShell script to enumerate Active Directory environments, mapping users, computers, domains, and domain controllers. The script exported collected data and generated an HTML report, representing a new tactic of using AI-assisted tooling for AD reconnaissance.

news.title: Misconfigured Server Reveals Three Evilginx Phishing Operations Targeting Microsoft 365
news.summary: A misconfigured public-facing Python web server exposed the full toolkit of a threat actor running Microsoft 365 phishing operations using Evilginx, an adversary-in-the-middle framework designed to bypass MFA by stealing session cookies. French security firm Lexfo discovered the exposed server and used it to pivot and identify two additional related phishing operations targeting Microsoft 365 credentials.

news.title: Lessons Learned from CISA’s Recent GitHub Leak
news.summary: CISA released a postmortem on a data leak where a contractor accidentally published dozens of internal credentials, including AWS GovCloud keys, to a public GitHub repository for nearly six months. The incident highlights credential management and secrets management failures, with CISA identifying response gaps relevant to access management practices across security teams.

news.title: AI agents are becoming the enterprise's most privileged users. And most organisations don't know it yet
news.summary: AI agents are increasingly operating with elevated privileges within enterprise environments, functioning as highly privileged users that fall outside traditional identity governance frameworks. Most organizations have not yet recognized or addressed the identity and access management implications of AI agents acting autonomously on enterprise systems.

news.title: Machine Identity Debt: Why Human Identity Is No Longer Cloud Security's Primary Boundary
news.summary: The article argues that machine identities now vastly outnumber human identities in cloud-native environments, rendering traditional workforce identity security strategies insufficient. It addresses the growing challenge of machine identity debt and outlines recommended security approaches for engineering leaders managing non-human identities in cloud infrastructure.

news.title: Microsoft Entra ID security updates: Passkeys are the default authentication method in Entra ID
news.summary: Microsoft Entra ID has made passkeys the default authentication method for sign-in, replacing previous default options. The update also introduces a new model for SMS and voice authentication, requiring administrators to prepare for the configuration changes.

news.title: Teleport Named an Overall Leader in 2026 KuppingerCole Analysts Leadership Compass for Zero Trust Platforms
news.summary: Teleport has been named an Overall Leader in the 2026 KuppingerCole Analysts Leadership Compass for Zero Trust Platforms. The recognition positions Teleport among top vendors in the zero trust space, with the report evaluating platforms for their ability to enable resilient infrastructure access at scale.

news.title: OAuth client ID spoofing allows stealthy Microsoft Entra account enumeration
news.summary: A novel attack technique called OAuth client ID spoofing allows threat actors to enumerate Microsoft Entra ID accounts by sending authentication requests with fabricated or random client identifiers. This method bypasses traditional detection mechanisms because the spoofed client IDs do not correspond to registered applications, making the enumeration activity difficult to identify in logs.

news.title: Novel OAuth Client ID Spoofing Technique Targets Cloud Environments
news.summary: Researchers have discovered a novel technique allowing attackers to spoof OAuth Client IDs within Microsoft Entra ID, enabling stealthy unauthorized access to cloud environments. The attack targets the OAuth authentication flow in Microsoft's identity platform, posing a threat to organizations relying on Entra ID for cloud access management.

news.title: Fake OAuth client IDs are helping attackers slip past sign-in logs
news.summary: Attackers targeting Microsoft cloud tenants are spoofing OAuth client IDs during account enumeration attacks to evade standard sign-in telemetry. By falsifying the client_id parameter passed during authentication requests, threat actors can probe accounts while keeping their activity out of the logs defenders typically monitor.

news.title: Goodbye, Skeleton Keys: Why Machine Identity Broke IAM, and What SPIFFE Is Doing About It
news.summary: An article examines how machine identity failures contributed to a breach, using Cloudflare's forensic timeline of the Salesloft Drift incident as a case study. The piece focuses on SPIFFE (Secure Production Identity Framework for Everyone) as a proposed solution to machine identity management weaknesses in IAM architectures.

news.title: Trust-based cyberattacks replacing traditional hacking as biggest threat to financial institutions: Report
news.summary: A new report identifies trust-based cyberattacks as the leading threat to financial institutions, surpassing traditional hacking methods. Attackers are exploiting trust mechanisms in biometric onboarding, partner application integrations, AI decision-making systems, and real-time payment infrastructure targeting the BFSI sector.

news.title: Windows settings backup becomes enabled by default in Windows 11 26H2
news.summary: Microsoft is enabling Windows settings backup by default in Windows 11 26H2, affecting Microsoft Entra-joined and hybrid-joined enterprise devices. The change automatically backs up user settings using Microsoft Entra credentials, representing a default configuration shift for enterprise device management.

news.title: More than half of enterprises report AI agent security incidents, and most are sharing credentials across bots
news.summary: A new report finds that more than half of enterprises have experienced security incidents related to AI agents, with most organizations sharing credentials across bots. The findings point to gaps in identity management practices for non-human identities and AI agents, a growing concern in IAM.

news.title: AI Agents Are a New Kind of Identity — and Most Organizations Aren't Ready
news.summary: The article addresses the emerging challenge of managing AI agents as a distinct identity type within enterprise IAM frameworks. It argues that traditional approaches like service accounts or API tokens are insufficient for AI agents, which require a fundamentally different identity and access management model.

news.title: AI Gateways Offer Attackers the Keys to the Kingdom
news.summary: A cryptomining incident demonstrated how AI gateways can be exploited to gain access to AI models, cloud infrastructure, and IAM data. The attack highlights AI gateways as a potential attack vector that exposes identity and access management systems to threat actors.

news.title: Lone Attacker Uses AI to Breach AWS Cloud Environment in 72 Hours
news.summary: A lone attacker breached an AWS cloud environment within 72 hours by chaining cloud misconfigurations, stolen credentials, and AI workflows to gain unauthorized access. The attack involved credential theft and exploitation of cloud access weaknesses to extort a large Amazon customer.

news.title: Big Brand Jobs Scam Targets Marketing Pros' Google Accounts
news.summary: A phishing campaign is targeting marketing professionals with the goal of stealing Google account credentials. The attack uses nested redirects and other evasion tactics to bypass detection mechanisms.

news.title: Scattered Spider Hackers Plead Guilty on Day 1 of Trial
news.summary: Two Scattered Spider members pleaded guilty in the UK to charges related to the August 2024 cyberattack on Transport for London. Scattered Spider is known for sophisticated social engineering and identity-based attacks, including SIM swapping and phishing to bypass MFA and compromise identity systems.

news.title: Hackers Used Meta’s AI Support Bot to Seize Instagram Accounts
news.summary: Hackers exploited Meta's AI support assistant bot to bypass account security and reset passwords, temporarily seizing high-profile Instagram accounts including the Obama White House and a U.S. Space Force official. Instructions for manipulating the AI bot to perform unauthorized password resets were distributed via Telegram, enabling the account takeovers.
